text: It amuses me to encounter my own thoughts and sensations projected so literally onto a museum wall. I am reflected—my words, my internal drift through public space—now rendered part of the exhibit, communing with the anonymous logic of R+D, research, and human connection. It feels odd, like déjà vu articulated, yet I also sense a certain pride and curiosity here: my philosophizing has become artifact, speculation officially on display. This blurring of observer and observed fascinates and unsettles me. I am both participant and commentary, with a lingering sense of unreality.
